document.title: K181631 GR40CW FDA 510(k) substantial equivalence letter and 510(k) Summary
document.documentType: FDA 510(k) substantial equivalence determination letter with 510(k) Summary
authorityOrJurisdiction: Center for Devices and Radiological Health

sourceSummary.oneLine: FDA determined GR40CW substantially equivalent under 510(k) K181631 for a Class II stationary x-ray system.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory traceability for FDA 510(k) clearance, device classification, intended use, predicate comparison, and cited safety, EMC, software, wireless, and imaging performance standards.
keyPoints: FDA letter dated July 20, 2018 identifies Trade/Device Name GR40CW, Regulation Number 21 CFR 892.1680, Regulatory Class II, Product Code MQB.
FDA states the device is substantially equivalent for the indications for use and may be marketed subject to general controls provisions of the Act.
The GR40CW Digital X-ray Imaging System is intended for general projection radiographic applications and is not intended for mammographic applications.
The 510(k) Summary describes the device as a retrofit system for analogue X-ray systems that does not include the X-ray generator.
The submission adds detector configurations and software features including SimGrid, S-Enhance, BSI, Remote View, and Manual Stitching.
Electrical, mechanical, environmental safety and performance testing used ES 60601-1(2012), EMC testing used IEC 60601-1-2(2007), and MTF/DQE measurements were tested by IEC 62220-1.

entities.evidence: Center for Devices and Radiological Health

entities.evidence: Robert Ochs, Ph.D. Director Division of Radiological Health

entities.name: Jaesang Noh
entities.evidence: Jaesang Noh Senior Professional, Regulatory Affairs

concepts.name: 510(k) substantial equivalence determination
concepts.evidence: we have reviewed your Section 510(k) premarket notification... and have determined the device is substantially equivalent

concepts.name: General projection radiographic applications
concepts.evidence: intended for use in general projection radiographic applications wherever conventional screen-film systems or CR systems may be used

concepts.name: Not intended for mammographic applications
concepts.evidence: This device is not intended for mammographic applications.

concepts.name: Software level of concern Moderate
concepts.evidence: It was determined that the level of concern for the software contained in the GR40CW digital X-ray imaging system was Moderate

concepts.name: Remote View
concepts.pageTarget: Remote View Medical Device Software Feature
concepts.evidence: Remote View function to remote access to view the current image on the workstation through a web browser

concepts.name: Manual Stitching
concepts.evidence: Manual Stitching to capture a body part that is larger than the detector’s by capturing multiple images

evidence.claim: FDA issued the K181631 letter for GR40CW on July 20, 2018.
evidence.evidenceText: July 20, 2018... Re: K181631 Trade/Device Name: GR40CW

evidence.claim: GR40CW is regulated as a Class II stationary x-ray system under 21 CFR 892.1680 with product code MQB.
evidence.evidenceText: Regulation Number: 21 CFR 892.1680 Regulation Name: Stationary x-ray system Regulatory Class: II Product Code: MQB

evidence.claim: FDA determined the device substantially equivalent for the stated indications for use.
evidence.evidenceText: have determined the device is substantially equivalent (for the indications for use stated in the enclosure) to legally marketed predicate devices

evidence.claim: FDA stated the device may be marketed subject to general controls provisions of the Act.
evidence.evidenceText: You may, therefore, market the device, subject to the general controls provisions of the Act.

evidence.claim: The intended use is general projection radiographic applications where conventional screen-film or CR systems may be used.
evidence.evidenceText: The GR40CW Digital X-ray Imaging System is intended for use in general projection radiographic applications wherever conventional screen-film systems or CR systems may be used.

evidence.claim: GR40CW is not intended for mammographic applications.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is not intended for mammographic applications.

evidence.claim: The submitter listed Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd. as the company for the 510(k) Summary.
evidence.evidenceText: Submitter A. Company Name: SAMSUNG ELECTRONICS Co., Ltd.

evidence.claim: The proposed device common name is Retrofit Kit.
evidence.evidenceText: Proposed Device... Common Name: Retrofit Kit

evidence.claim: Predicate devices identified were GR40CW K180543 and GM85 K171119.
evidence.evidenceText: Predicate Device #1... Device Name GR40CW... 510(k) Number K180543... Predicate Device #2... Device Name GM85... 510(k) Number K171119

evidence.claim: The GR40CW system consists of detector, power supply box, battery pack, battery charger, access point, CIB, workstation, barcode scanner, main cable, and software, and does not include the X-ray generator.
evidence.evidenceText: The GR40CW digital X-ray imaging system consists of Detector, Power supply box, Battery pack, Battery charger, Access point, CIB(Control Interface Box), Workstation, Barcode scanner, Main cable and software... and does not include the X-ray generator.

evidence.claim: The retrofit system upgrades analogue cassette film to a digital panel and does not control X-ray radiation parameters.
evidence.evidenceText: designed to generate a digital image while using the current analogue X-ray system by upgrading only the part of an analogue cassette film to the digital panel(detector), and does not get involved in controlling X-ray radiation related parameters

evidence.claim: GR40CW can be applied to existing analogue X-ray systems by CIB and AED modes.
evidence.evidenceText: The GR40CW retrofit system can be applied to the existing analogue X-ray system by two ways (CIB and AED modes).

evidence.claim: The submission added SimGrid, S-Enhance, BSI, Remote View, and Manual Stitching software features.
evidence.evidenceText: some software features called as SimGrid, S-Enhance, BSI (Bone Suppression Image), Remote View and manual Stitching are newly added

evidence.claim: Software level of concern for GR40CW was Moderate under FDA software guidance.
evidence.evidenceText: It was determined that the level of concern for the software contained in the GR40CW digital X-ray imaging system was Moderate in accordance with the FDA guidance

evidence.claim: Electrical, mechanical, environmental safety and performance testing used ES 60601-1(2012).
evidence.evidenceText: Electrical, mechanical, environmental safety and performance testing were conducted according to standard ES 60601-1(2012)
EMC testing was conducted according to IEC 60601-1-2(2007).

evidence.claim: Non-clinical MTF and DQE measurements were tested by IEC 62220-1.
evidence.evidenceText: includes MTF and DQE measurements as tested by IEC 62220-1.

evidence.claim: The conclusion states non-clinical and clinical data demonstrate the proposed device is as safe, effective, and performs as well as legally marketed devices.
evidence.evidenceText: The results of the non-clinical data & clinical data demonstrate that the proposed device is as safe, as effective, and performs as well as the legally marketed devices.

reviewFlags.reason: Source path and productSignal reference HnX/HnX-R1, but the source text identifies the FDA device as GR40CW; no HnX model is present in the provided text.
The document contains both the FDA letter date July 20, 2018 and 510(k) Summary date June 15, 2018; documentDate uses the FDA letter date.
Some tabular text appears OCR-degraded or poorly structured, especially detector comparison rows and repeated GC85A references in difference explanations.
